source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Enable Security Posture

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Enable Security Posture
input_row.rationale: The security posture dashboard provides insights about your workload security posture at the runtime phase of the software delivery life-cycle. Impact: GKE security posture configuration auditing checks your workloads against a set of defined best practices. Each configuration check has its own impact or risk. Learn more about the checks: https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/about- configuration-scanning Example: The host namespace check identifies pods that share host namespaces. Pods that share host namespaces allow Pod processes to communicate with host processes and gather host information, which could lead to a container escape
input_row.audit: Check the SecurityPostureConfig on your cluster: gcloud container clusters --location describe securityPostureConfig: mode: BASIC
input_row.remediation: Enable security posture via the UI, gCloud or API. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/protect-workload-configuration Default Value: GKE security posture has multiple features. Not all are on by default. Configuration auditing is enabled by default for new standard and autopilot clusters. securityPostureConfig: mode: BASIC References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/about-security- posture-dashboard
input_row.impact: GKE security posture configuration auditing checks your workloads against a set of defined best practices. Each configuration check has its own impact or risk. Learn more about the checks: https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/about- configuration-scanning Example: The host namespace check identifies pods that share host namespaces. Pods that share host namespaces allow Pod processes to communicate with host processes and gather host information, which could lead to a container escape

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be verified using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) based on the "audit" field indication that specifies a `gcloud` command to check the `SecurityPostureConfig`.
- As this involves checking the configuration of a GKE cluster's security posture, it is a standard GCP service property and is exposed through GCP APIs.
- The audit mentions the exact command needed to check this configuration, ensuring that it can be automated.
- This does not require any manual inspection, SSH access, or human judgment for evaluation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_security_posture_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`) command to describe the `SecurityPostureConfig` for GKE clusters.
- Command to be used: `gcloud container clusters describe --location=<CLUSTER_LOCATION> <CLUSTER_NAME> --format="value(securityPostureConfig.mode)"`
- Enumerate all GKE clusters within the organization or specific project.
- Validate that the `securityPostureConfig.mode` is set to `BASIC`.
- Pass/Fail criteria: The cluster should have its `securityPostureConfig` mode set to `BASIC` or higher. If the mode is not set or is lower than `BASIC`, it fails the check.
